creator: bcran@FreeBSD.org
text:
----------  Forwarded Message  ----------

Subject: Re: misc/142335: Download of Release 8.0 LIVE is NOT a "live" from CD 
program.
Date: Thursday 04 March 2010
From: Diana Allen <smokepole32@yahoo.com>
To: Bruce Cran <bruce@cran.org.uk>

Actually, I do not remember whether there was a "fix it" on the menu or not.
I just know that there was NO information referring me to the "fix it" entry, 
and that starting at the top of the menu wanted to partition and install.
Perhaps, you should CHANGE the program to provide "live" directly, and ONLY 
call the "installer" for installs!  THIS is what ALL the other "live" programs 
do!
When it would NOT run "live" I threw the download disc away since I did NOT 
want to do an install at that time, and therefore have no way to check it.  
(Note that I have over 150 different versions of Linux, going back at least 10 
years, and did NOT need to keep something that was obviously NOT working as it 
should have, for whatever reason; INCLUDING LACK OF INSTRUCTIONS!)

--- On Wed, 3/3/10, Bruce Cran <bruce@cran.org.uk> wrote:

> From: Bruce Cran <bruce@cran.org.uk>
> Subject: Re: misc/142335: Download of Release 8.0 LIVE is NOT a "live" from 
CD program.
> To: "Diana Allen" <smokepole32@yahoo.com>
> Date: Wednesday, March 3, 2010, 12:58 PM
> On Wednesday 03 March 2010 15:46:57
> you wrote:
> > The installer INSISTS on partitioning the HD BEFORE
> proceeding!  THIS does
> >  NOT constitute an "acceptable" "live" CD program
> from MY point of view!
> 
> I agree - the installer shouldn't be wanting to change the
> disk at all in 
> livefs mode. Just to confirm, when the installer starts do
> you scroll down to 
> "FixIt", hit enter and the disk partitioning page gets
> displayed?
> 
> -- 
> Bruce Cran
